pages.text: In the beginning, heaven and earth opened; the sun traveled back and forth, the four seasons alternated, cold and heat succeeded each other. Thus arose the great cycle, divided into twenty-four parts.$(br)$(br)The way of heaven is such that even at its peak, it does not last long — a slight delay, and it begins to wane.$(br)$(br)Therefore, the Summer Solstice is not the hottest time, and the Winter Solstice is not the coldest day.
